entities.name: CAJANUS PLATYCARPUS
entities.description: A wild species of Cajanus used in interspecific crossing.

entities.name: EMBRYO RESCUE
entities.description: A technique used to rescue embryos from incompatible interspecific crosses to produce fertile progeny.

entities.name: TISSUE CULTURE
entities.description: Techniques used to grow plant cells or tissues in vitro to produce fertile progeny.

entities.name: FLOWER COLOUR
entities.description: The color of the flowers in the resulting progeny.

entities.name: FLOWER MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical structure and form of the flowers in the resulting progeny.

entities.name: SEED COLOUR
entities.description: The color of the seeds in the resulting progeny.

entities.name: SEED MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical structure and form of the seeds in the resulting progeny.

entities.name: POLLEN FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of the pollen to successfully fertilize an egg.

entities.name: BACKCROSS GENERATIONS
entities.description: The BC2F1, BC3F1, and BC4F1 generations produced through backcrossing.

entities.name: TERTIARY GENE POOL
entities.description: A group of wild species that are distantly related to the crop species and difficult to cross.

entities.name: INTERSPECIFIC INCOMPATIBLE CROSS
entities.description: A cross between two different species that typically fails to produce viable or fertile offspring.

relationships.description: Used to overcome incompatibility in the cross
Used to produce fertile progeny from the cross
Belongs to the tertiary gene pool of Cajanus
